title: Talk to the Machine: Prompt Engineering
modules: Why Your First Prompt Always Fails
Roles, Rules, and Constraints That Work
Chaining Prompts to Solve Hard Problems
Design a Prompt That Teaches Someone Else
synopsis: You've been hired as an AI prompt consultant for three very different clients — a musician, a scientist, and a city planner — and none of their AI tools are doing what they need yet. You have to diagnose why and fix it, one prompt at a time.
rationale: Prompt engineering is rapidly becoming a foundational workplace skill, yet teens are mostly using AI reactively rather than learning to direct it with precision and intentionality.
learning_outcome: Students can write, iterate, and chain prompts using role assignment, constraints, and output formatting to reliably extract useful, accurate results from a general-purpose AI tool.
source_signals: Modern-Day Oracles or Bullshit Machines? How to thrive in a ChatGPT world
How to learn AI for students & professionals (guide)
AI Complete OneShot Course for Beginners | Learn AI & ML Fundamentals from Scratch
